reviews.review_title: Nice idea spoiled by rough controls
reviews.review_description:
I like the idea of extending Minesweeper into the third dimension, but this fails in two fundamental ways:
1. The controls are really fiddly. I was forever triggering the hand-swap gesture while playing, and couldn't figure out the planar view thing.
2. You just can't see enough! There's a moment when the grid is shrinking down to transition between full/plane views, and you get a momentary glimpse through the whole thing. Why can't this be the normal view? If the grid were spaced out so you could see through it, and maybe the revealed areas could also be partially transparent, it could work so much better.

Also, see Hexceed (free on Steam) for how to fix all the game design flaws from the original Minesweeper!

There is some wiz-bang feature where you click your controllers together to swap left and right hands. Nifty! Except that this should be a one-time set up in the options, not something you would want to keep doing during game play. Which is what constantly happens, the left and right keep swapping, regardless of whether you clink the controllers together. And it's irritating enough to eventually ruin the game. Otherwise, this is a decent game, although getting used to the controls takes time. Oh yeah, I don't know why every time I start a game, the cube is inches over my head, instead of in front of me. Lots of potential, not enough thought into interface design.

If you like minesweeper this is a must download. If you are looking for a good game to relax to this is a must download. It’s a very fun take on minesweeper. There are a few problems with controls being a little inconvenient and extreme mode lagging immensely, but nothing that ruins the game. In the end it is a free game and you have nothing to lose from downloading it.

reviews.review_title: Fun game, but confusing at first
reviews.review_description: A very fun spin on minesweeper. My only complaint is that there is no tutorial, so it took me a while to figure it out. For anyone else who gets confused, when you're looking at the whole grid, the numbers show how many bombs are touching them in any of the three dimensions, not just for that plane.  But when you are looking at a single plane, the numbers just represent how many are touching on that plane.

It is hard to see the cube. There is a need to have at least a way to look around the cube to see from all perspective. Because you cannot see inside the cube, it is hard to know what is safe to click.

Yes, the user interface is quite confusing at first - or if you play again after some time. Persistent, and also Global, High scores are missing, which is a shame. But really, it is not too bad. What is relevant to me, is that this app truly uses 3D and VR, and is not just a 2d puzzler with fancy graphics. It is a 3d Puzzler with, well, vintage looking, working for the purpose, graphics. Possibly an even abstract look would give it a more modern appearance.

I reeeeeally wanted to like this. I really did. I grew up on this game. I did my best to figure out how the controllers work, but never got close. You know those games where you press all buttons randomly to see if one of them does something? And nothing happens? ... like that.  
In the end I had to delete it. 
Go ahead and try it. See if you're able to get it to work. Hopefully you can. 
Thank you to the developer for giving it a good faith effort.

reviews.review_title: Just get to know the game!
reviews.review_description:
At first I was agreeing with all the other reviews with all the control stuff, but then once I actually looked at the controls and fiddled around with them, it actually turned out to be pretty fun! I just got done with my first 8×8×8 cube and thought it was really fun!

Listen, if you just try it out and mess around a little, the controls will feel natural and you'll become addicted!
